name: slot_filtered_serving
description: Declares a network starting at slot 0 with a peer holding a 20-entry chain (slots 138-157). Advances the imaginary clock to slot 145 via advance_to_slot, then serves chain-sync to one client. Because current_slot is 145, only entries with slot <= 145 are visible — that's 8 entries (slots 138-145, block_numbers 0-7). The client sees 8 RollForward messages and then AwaitReply at tip. This demonstrates that slot-aware serve is opt-in via the network time model: without a network declaration (no start_slot), all entries are visible.

peers.description: A block producer whose chain is only partially visible at the current slot
